title: Boot test_blink diagnostic firmware
description: Prove lichen/apps/test_blink runs on its declared T1000-E hardware target using an independent observable LED/USB/serial oracle. If the exact hardware is unavailable, record BLOCKED with the required device/observer instead of treating a build as proof.
acceptance_criteria: 1. T1000-E image builds with recorded hash. 2. Physical execution is independently observed after reset, or the issue records a hardware BLOCKED result with exact requirement. 3. Flash command, device identity, observation, and logs are recorded. 4. Any temporary bench state is restored.
notes:
Source base 8e02a7ae924a226c91c23d8d19f0f5350c248364 plus current scoped workspace fingerprint d22f4eb7467bbb5955175c2e504137d1a1cd3d1fad779766a76d3ed5763bd8f8. Builder i-03d978911e97d4350, ARM64 us-west-2c. Build command: west build -p always -b t1000_e/nrf52840 -s /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-m5m1/lichen/apps/test_blink -d /mnt/lichen-zephyr/validation/project-LICHEN-m5m1/test-blink-t1000e -- -DBOARD_ROOT=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-m5m1/lichen -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-m5m1/lichen. Build WIN only, not boot proof. Standalone 0x1000 BIN SHA-256 3a80a56d29fe8a9841568635bb5844c61e80b67bb37b70dd87d399cdb4980c12; ELF SHA-256 e26447118cfb6413e5f38c3335e65122c88988cfbddeb9a8c0ecf0cd742d081b. Retained local BIN /var/folders/pg/t21pc_0j6_3bq40d087mjbrw0000gn/T/opencode/test-blink-t1000e.bin same hash; build log test-blink-t1000e-build.log SHA-256 b5059a8fe83090cfb3cbd185ba91e2d0d6ca993bc5de7617ef9c60b7c6403a4f. Module-isolation verification passed. PHYSICAL RESULT BLOCKED: no identified T1000-E/DFU device is attached; current USB serial inventory contains only unrelated /dev/tty.usbserial-A704ESXG. Flashing is destructive and lacks explicit authorization. Required command after double-tap DFU and identifying <DFU_PORT>: adafruit-nrfutil dfu genpkg --dev-type 0x0052 --application /var/folders/pg/t21pc_0j6_3bq40d087mjbrw0000gn/T/opencode/test-blink-t1000e.bin /tmp/test-blink-t1000e.zip; adafruit-nrfutil --verbose dfu serial --package /tmp/test-blink-t1000e.zip -p <DFU_PORT> -b 115200 --singlebank. Required oracle: after reset, P0.24 LED alternates approximately one second on/one second off continuously; USB/console is intentionally disabled. Required cleanup: restore normal signed MCUboot+puck firmware with ./flash-t1000e.sh and verify normal device identity. Follow-up project-LICHEN-902l tracks authorization, device identity, observation, and restoration. No bench state was changed.
REVIEW SAFETY CORRECTIONS: retained dated USB inventory /var/folders/pg/t21pc_0j6_3bq40d087mjbrw0000gn/T/opencode/test-blink-usb-inventory.log SHA-256 7fe5326e6543ca1f075f7865ed91983e072c41ef94e63bdcfda41bf434124d68. Physical oracle is continuous periodic P0.24 alternation; no one-second cadence is claimed without measurement. Before any flash, capture current firmware identity and a restorable artifact/hash. Restoration must separately trigger or double-reset into DFU, wait, rediscover the T1000-E /dev/serial/by-id port, then run adafruit-nrfutil dfu serial without --touch and require transfer progress plus Device programmed before verifying restored identity. flash-t1000e.sh was fixed to use this sequence instead of reopening the pre-reset path with --touch. The test_blink overlay comment now correctly identifies boot_partition as 0x1000. No flash or bench mutation occurred.
Final flash-t1000e.sh safety fix also restricts automatic app discovery to touch-capable LICHEN if00, rejects ambiguous app/DFU matches before selection, preserves serial identity when rediscovering DFU, polls up to 20 seconds, and requires explicit T1000E_PORT_STATE=app|dfu for unrecognized overrides. bash -n and git diff --check pass; final independent script review found no findings.
close_reason: Built and hashed standalone T1000-E test-blink image at 0x1000; retained build and dated USB inventory evidence; classified physical execution BLOCKED under project-LICHEN-902l because no identified device or flash authorization exists. Corrected stale address documentation and made serial DFU restoration safe across USB re-enumeration. Reviews converged with no findings.
